Package com.team81

Class DatabaseManager

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.team81.DatabaseManager
  • public class DatabaseManager
extends java.lang.Object
    <<<<<<< Updated upstream
    Manages the database connection to the PostgreSQL server using the Singleton pattern. This class provides a single shared Connection instance for the entire application, reading credentials from environment variables or a .env file via the dotenv library.

    Usage example:

       Connection conn = DatabaseManager.getConnection();
   // use conn for queries ...
   DatabaseManager.closeConnection();
    =======
    Manages a singleton database connection to the team's PostgreSQL database hosted on AWS. Credentials are loaded from environment variables or a .env file via the dotenv library. Only one active connection is maintained at a time by enforcing the Singleton design pattern.
    >>>>>>> Stashed changes

      Constructors 
      Modifier Constructor Description
      private DatabaseManager() <<<<<<< Updated upstream
      Private constructor to enforce the Singleton pattern.
      =======
      Private constructor to prevent external instantiation, enforcing the Singleton pattern via the static getConnection() method.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      static void closeConnection() <<<<<<< Updated upstream
      Closes the shared database connection if it is currently open.
      =======
      Closes the singleton database Connection if it is currently open and sets the internal reference to null so that a fresh connection will be created on the next call to getConnection().
      >>>>>>> Stashed changes
      static java.sql.Connection getConnection() <<<<<<< Updated upstream
      Returns the shared database connection, creating it if necessary.
      =======
      Returns the singleton database Connection.
      >>>>>>> Stashed changes

    • Field Detail

      • dotenv

        private static final io.github.cdimascio.dotenv.Dotenv dotenv
        <<<<<<< Updated upstream
        Dotenv configuration, ignoring a missing .env file.
        =======
        Dotenv instance used to load configuration values from a .env file or from the system environment. Configured to silently ignore a missing file so the application can still start if the environment variables are provided some other way.
        >>>>>>> Stashed changes

      • HOST

        private static final java.lang.String HOST
        <<<<<<< Updated upstream
        Database host, defaults to the TAMU engineering server.
        =======
        Hostname of the PostgreSQL server. Defaults to csce-315-db.engr.tamu.edu when not overridden by the DB_HOST environment variable.
        >>>>>>> Stashed changes

      • DATABASE

        private static final java.lang.String DATABASE
        <<<<<<< Updated upstream
        Database name, defaults to team_81_db.
        =======
        Name of the database to connect to. Defaults to team_81_db when not overridden by the DB_NAME environment variable.
        >>>>>>> Stashed changes

      • USER

        private static final java.lang.String USER
        <<<<<<< Updated upstream
        Database user, defaults to team_81.
        =======
        Database username. Defaults to team_81 when not overridden by the DB_USER environment variable.
        >>>>>>> Stashed changes

      • PASSWORD

        private static final java.lang.String PASSWORD
        <<<<<<< Updated upstream
        Database password read from environment or .env file.
        =======
        Database password read from the DB_PASSWORD environment variable or .env file. No default is provided for security reasons.
        >>>>>>> Stashed changes

      • URL

        private static final java.lang.String URL
        <<<<<<< Updated upstream
        Full JDBC URL constructed from host, port, and database name.
        =======
        JDBC connection URL constructed from the configured host and database name, using the default PostgreSQL port 5432.
        >>>>>>> Stashed changes

      • connection

        private static java.sql.Connection connection
        <<<<<<< Updated upstream
        The single shared database connection instance.
        =======
        The single shared JDBC Connection instance. May be null if no connection has been established yet or if the connection has been explicitly closed.
        >>>>>>> Stashed changes

    • Constructor Detail

      • DatabaseManager

        private DatabaseManager()
        <<<<<<< Updated upstream
        Private constructor to enforce the Singleton pattern.
        =======
        Private constructor to prevent external instantiation, enforcing the Singleton pattern via the static getConnection() method.
        >>>>>>> Stashed changes

    • Method Detail

      • getConnection

        public static java.sql.Connection getConnection()
        <<<<<<< Updated upstream
        Returns the shared database connection, creating it if necessary. If the connection has not been established yet, this method connects to the PostgreSQL database using the configured credentials and URL.
        Returns:
        the shared Connection instance, or null if the connection failed
        =======
        Returns the singleton database Connection. If no connection exists yet, one is created using the configured JDBC URL and credentials. Subsequent calls return the same connection instance.
        Returns:
        the active database Connection, or null if the connection attempt failed
        >>>>>>> Stashed changes

      • closeConnection

        public static void closeConnection()
        <<<<<<< Updated upstream
        Closes the shared database connection if it is currently open. After this method returns, getConnection() will create a new connection on the next call.
        =======
        Closes the singleton database Connection if it is currently open and sets the internal reference to null so that a fresh connection will be created on the next call to getConnection().
        >>>>>>> Stashed changes
